feedback.details: Diagram validates successfully with mmdc (exit 0) and renders without errors. No issues found.

Diagram contains exactly: 1 system box (llm-switch at line 8), 2 Person_Ext boxes (Developers at line 6, Operations Engineers at line 7), and exactly 6 System_Ext boxes (Nomad, Consul, Vault, localmodels, frontierapis, observability at lines 9-14). No container or component boxes present.

All 6 external systems have labeled relationships with protocols: nomad (Nomad job specification, line 18), consul (HTTP/DNS, line 19), vault (HTTP/TLS, line 20), localmodels (HTTP/gRPC, line 21), frontierapis (HTTP, line 22), observability (HTTP/gRPC, line 23). No unlabeled connections.

File contains exactly four H2 sections with adequate word counts: 'System Context' (line 29, ~170 words), 'User Roles' (line 34, ~130 words), 'External Dependencies' (line 38, ~140 words), 'Key Interactions' (line 42, ~160 words). All exceed minimum thresholds.

All diagram elements include PRD references (e.g., [PRD-Executive Summary] line 8, [PRD-User Journeys] lines 6-7). Narrative paragraphs end with PRD references. Minor deduction for potential gaps in granular sentence-level attribution.

Narrative explicitly mentions all 7 required technologies: Golang ('Go service' line 30), Docker (line 30), Nomad (line 30, 34), Consul (line 30, 34), Vault (line 30, 34), vLLM (line 30, 36), langfuse (line 36, 39). Diagram uses correct C4 shapes (hexagon System at line 8, Person_Ext at lines 6-7, System_Ext at lines 9-14).

Critical gaps in production-readiness documentation. (1) Zero-code-change: covered (line 34). (2) Multiple model types: covered (lines 30, 36, 42). (3) Nomad constraints: covered (line 42: 'placement constraints favoring GPU-equipped nodes'). (4) API timeout scenarios: NOT covered - no mention of timeout configuration, circuit breakers, or timeout handling. (5) Network partition tolerance: NOT covered - no mention of Consul/Vault partition behavior, failover, HA, or split-brain scenarios. These omissions represent fundamental production gaps.
File begins with H1 '# C1 System Context' (line 1), followed immediately by ```mermaid block (line 3), followed by narrative sections (line 29+). No extraneous text before diagram.

All diagram labels match narrative exactly: 'Developers' (line 6, 34), 'Operations Engineers' (line 7, 34), 'llm-switch' (line 8, 30), 'Nomad Cluster' (line 9, 38), 'Consul' (line 10, 38), 'Vault' (line 11, 38), 'Local Model Servers' (line 12, 38), 'Frontier API Providers' (line 13, 38), 'Observability Tools' (line 14, 38). No abbreviation discrepancies.

overall_summary: The C1 System Context document is well-structured with valid Mermaid syntax, correct C4 level 1 composition (1 system, 2 people, 6 external systems), complete narrative sections meeting word count requirements, and strong PRD traceability. Technology stack compliance is complete with all 7 required technologies documented. However, the document has a critical gap in Edge Case Coverage: it completely omits API timeout scenarios (timeout configuration, circuit breakers, retry logic) and network partition tolerance (Consul/Vault split-brain handling, failover behavior). These are fundamental production requirements for any distributed system deployed in Nomad clusters. Without these specifications, the design cannot be considered production-ready despite its other strengths. The diagram itself has a minor syntax issue at line 14 (missing closing parenthesis on System_Ext for observability), though mmdc validates leniently.
